name: Mock City Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb boneless Veal, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
1/2 lb boneless Pork, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
1/2 lb boneless Beef, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
1 tbsp vegetable oil (or other oil for frying)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Pat all cubes dry with paper towels. Place flour in a zip-loc plastic bag, seasoned bread crumbs in another zip-loc bag.
3. Coat each meat cube first in the flour by shaking it well, then dip it in beaten egg wash until fully covered. Next, place the cube in the seasoned bread crumbs bag and shake to ensure an even coating.
4. Insert the coated cube onto a skewer (traditionally at least 1/2-inch long).
5. Repeat steps 3-4 with remaining cubes, alternating between veal, beef, and pork if using all three meats.
6. Heat oil in a large heavy frying pan until hot. Brown the meat skewers on all sides without burning them.
7. Remove from the frying pan and place on a lightly oiled baking sheet. Bake at 350 degrees F for 45 minutes or until desired doneness is reached.
Notes:
These dishes are considered comfort food, evoking positive feelings for some people. This recipe can also be made with any combination of the meats listed or just one type, though it won't be exactly like real chicken if using boneless chicken cubes instead. Ensuring an even coating of bread crumbs is crucial for the success of this dish.
